column1: title#shloka_in_sanskrit#transliteration_en#translation_en#word_by_word_en

BG 14.1#श्रीभगवानुवाच
परं भूय: प्रवक्ष्यामि ज्ञानानां ज्ञानमुत्तमम्
यज्ज्ञात्वा मुनय: सर्वे परां सिद्धिमितो गता:#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha
paraṁ bhūyaḥ pravakṣhyāmi jñānānāṁ jñānam uttamam
yaj jñātvā munayaḥ sarve parāṁ siddhim ito gatāḥ#The Divine Lord said: I shall once again explain to you the supreme wisdom, the best of all knowledge
column2: by knowing which, all the great saints attained the highest perfection.#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha—the Divine Lord said

column1: BG 14.2#इदं ज्ञानमुपाश्रित्य मम साधर्म्यमागता:\nसर्गेऽपि नोपजायन्ते प्रलये न व्यथन्ति च#idaṁ jñānam upāśhritya mama sādharmyam āgatāḥ\nsarge ’pi nopajāyante pralaye na vyathanti cha#Those who take refuge in this wisdom will be united with Me. They will not be reborn at the time of creation nor destroyed at the time of dissolution.#idam—this

BG 14.3#मम योनिर्महद् ब्रह्म तस्मिन्गर्भं दधाम्यहम्
सम्भव: सर्वभूतानां ततो भवति भारत
सर्वयोनिषु कौन्तेय मूर्तय: सम्भवन्ति या:
तासां ब्रह्म महद्योनिरहं बीजप्रद: पिता#mama yonir mahad brahma tasmin garbhaṁ dadhāmy aham
sambhavaḥ sarva-bhūtānāṁ tato bhavati bhārata
sarva-yoniṣhu kaunteya mūrtayaḥ sambhavanti yāḥ
tāsāṁ brahma mahad yonir ahaṁ bīja-pradaḥ pitā#BG 14.3-4:#mama—my
column3: mahat brahma—the total material substance, prakṛiti

column1:
BG 14.4#मम योनिर्महद् ब्रह्म तस्मिन्गर्भं दधाम्यहम्
सम्भव: सर्वभूतानां ततो भवति भारत
सर्वयोनिषु कौन्तेय मूर्तय: सम्भवन्ति या:
तासां ब्रह्म महद्योनिरहं बीजप्रद: पिता#mama yonir mahad brahma tasmin garbhaṁ dadhāmy aham
sambhavaḥ sarva-bhūtānāṁ tato bhavati bhārata
sarva-yoniṣhu kaunteya mūrtayaḥ sambhavanti yāḥ
tāsāṁ brahma mahad yonir ahaṁ bīja-pradaḥ pitā#BG 14.3-4:#mama—my
column3: mahat brahma—the total material substance, prakṛiti

column1: BG 14.5#सत्त्वं रजस्तम इति गुणा: प्रकृतिसम्भवा:\nनिबध्नन्ति महाबाहो देहे देहिनमव्ययम्#sattvaṁ rajas tama iti guṇāḥ prakṛiti-sambhavāḥ\nnibadhnanti mahā-bāho dehe dehinam avyayam#O mighty-armed Arjun, the material energy consists of three guṇas (modes)—sattva (goodness), rajas (passion), and tamas (ignorance). These modes bind the eternal soul to the perishable body.#sattvam—mode of goodness

BG 14.6#तत्र सत्त्वं निर्मलत्वात्प्रकाशकमनामयम्\nसुखसङ्गेन बध्नाति ज्ञानसङ्गेन चानघ#tatra sattvaṁ nirmalatvāt prakāśhakam anāmayam\nsukha-saṅgena badhnāti jñāna-saṅgena chānagha#Amongst these, sattva guṇa, the mode of goodness, being purer than the others, is illuminating and full of well-being. O sinless one, it binds the soul by creating attachment for a sense of happiness and knowledge.#tatra—amongst these

BG 14.7#रजो रागात्मकं विद्धि तृष्णासङ्गसमुद्भवम्\nतन्निबध्नाति कौन्तेय कर्मसङ्गेन देहिनम्#rajo rāgātmakaṁ viddhi tṛiṣhṇā-saṅga-samudbhavam\ntan nibadhnāti kaunteya karma-saṅgena dehinam#O Arjun, rajo guṇa is of the nature of passion. It arises from worldly desires and affections, and binds the soul through attachment to fruitive actions.#rajaḥ—mode of passion

BG 14.8#तमस्त्वज्ञानजं विद्धि मोहनं सर्वदेहिनाम्\nप्रमादालस्यनिद्राभिस्तन्निबध्नाति भारत#tamas tv ajñāna-jaṁ viddhi mohanaṁ sarva-dehinām\npramādālasya-nidrābhis tan nibadhnāti bhārata#O Arjun, tamo guṇa, which is born of ignorance, is the cause of illusion for the embodied souls. It deludes all living beings through negligence, laziness, and sleep.#tamaḥ—mode of ignorance

BG 14.9#सत्त्वं सुखे सञ्जयति रज: कर्मणि भारत\nज्ञानमावृत्य तु तम: प्रमादे सञ्जयत्युत#sattvaṁ sukhe sañjayati rajaḥ karmaṇi bhārata\njñānam āvṛitya tu tamaḥ pramāde sañjayaty uta#Sattva binds one to material happiness
column2: rajas conditions the soul toward actions
column3: and tamas clouds wisdom and binds one to delusion.#sattvam—mode of goodness

column1: BG 14.10#रजस्तमश्चाभिभूय सत्त्वं भवति भारत\nरज: सत्त्वं तमश्चैव तम: सत्त्वं रजस्तथा#rajas tamaśh chābhibhūya sattvaṁ bhavati bhārata\nrajaḥ sattvaṁ tamaśh chaiva tamaḥ sattvaṁ rajas tathā#Sometimes goodness (sattva) prevails over passion (rajas) and ignorance (tamas), O scion of Bharat. Sometimes passion (rajas) dominates goodness (sattva) and ignorance (tamas), and at other times ignorance (tamas) overcomes goodness (sattva) and passion (rajas).#rajaḥ—mode of passion

BG 14.11#सर्वद्वारेषु देहेऽस्मिन्प्रकाश उपजायते
ज्ञानं यदा तदा विद्याद्विवृद्धं सत्त्वमित्युत
लोभ: प्रवृत्तिरारम्भ: कर्मणामशम: स्पृहा
रजस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे भरतर्षभ
अप्रकाशोऽप्रवृत्तिश्च प्रमादो मोह एव च
तमस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे कुरुनन्दन#sarva-dvāreṣhu dehe ’smin prakāśha upajāyate
jñānaṁ yadā tadā vidyād vivṛiddhaṁ sattvam ity uta
lobhaḥ pravṛittir ārambhaḥ karmaṇām aśhamaḥ spṛihā
rajasy etāni jāyante vivṛiddhe bharatarṣhabha
aprakāśho ’pravṛittiśh cha pramādo moha eva cha
tamasy etāni jāyante vivṛiddhe kuru-nandana#BG 14.11-13:#sarva—all
column25: bharata-ṛiṣhabha—the best of the Bharatas, Arjun
column37: kuru-nandana—the joy of the Kurus, Arjun

column1:
BG 14.12#सर्वद्वारेषु देहेऽस्मिन्प्रकाश उपजायते
ज्ञानं यदा तदा विद्याद्विवृद्धं सत्त्वमित्युत
लोभ: प्रवृत्तिरारम्भ: कर्मणामशम: स्पृहा

तमस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे कुरुनन्दन#sarva-dvāreṣhu dehe ’smin prakāśha upajāyate
jñānaṁ yadā tadā vidyād vivṛiddhaṁ sattvam ity uta

aprakāśho ’pravṛittiśh cha pramādo moha eva cha
tamasy etāni jāyante vivṛiddhe kuru-nandana#BG 14.11-13:#sarva—all

column37: kuru-nandana—the joy of the Kurus, Arjun

ज्ञानं यदा तदा विद्याद्विवृद्धं सत्त्वमित्युत
लोभ: प्रवृत्तिरारम्भ: कर्मणामशम: स्पृहा

अप्रकाशोऽप्रवृत्तिश्च प्रमादो मोह एव च
तमस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे कुरुनन्दन#sarva-dvāreṣhu dehe ’smin prakāśha upajāyate
jñānaṁ yadā tadā vidyād vivṛiddhaṁ sattvam ity uta

aprakāśho ’pravṛittiśh cha pramādo moha eva cha

column37: kuru-nandana—the joy of the Kurus, Arjun

column1:
BG 14.14#यदा सत्त्वे प्रवृद्धे तु प्रलयं याति देहभृत्
तदोत्तमविदां लोकानमलान्प्रतिपद्यते
रजसि प्रलयं गत्वा कर्मसङ्गिषु जायते
तथा प्रलीनस्तमसि मूढयोनिषु जायते#yadā sattve pravṛiddhe tu pralayaṁ yāti deha-bhṛit
tadottama-vidāṁ lokān amalān pratipadyate
rajasi pralayaṁ gatvā karma-saṅgiṣhu jāyate
tathā pralīnas tamasi mūḍha-yoniṣhu jāyate#BG 14.14-15:#yadā—when

BG 14.15#यदा सत्त्वे प्रवृद्धे तु प्रलयं याति देहभृत्
तदोत्तमविदां लोकानमलान्प्रतिपद्यते
रजसि प्रलयं गत्वा कर्मसङ्गिषु जायते
तथा प्रलीनस्तमसि मूढयोनिषु जायते#yadā sattve pravṛiddhe tu pralayaṁ yāti deha-bhṛit
tadottama-vidāṁ lokān amalān pratipadyate
rajasi pralayaṁ gatvā karma-saṅgiṣhu jāyate
tathā pralīnas tamasi mūḍha-yoniṣhu jāyate#BG 14.14-15:#yadā—when

BG 14.16#कर्मण: सुकृतस्याहु: सात्त्विकं निर्मलं फलम्\nरजसस्तु फलं दु:खमज्ञानं तमस: फलम्#karmaṇaḥ sukṛitasyāhuḥ sāttvikaṁ nirmalaṁ phalam\nrajasas tu phalaṁ duḥkham ajñānaṁ tamasaḥ phalam#It is said the fruit of actions performed in the mode of goodness bestow pure results. Actions done in the mode of passion result in pain, while those performed in the mode of ignorance result in darkness.#karmaṇaḥ—of action

BG 14.17#सत्त्वात्सञ्जायते ज्ञानं रजसो लोभ एव च\nप्रमादमोहौ तमसो भवतोऽज्ञानमेव च#sattvāt sañjāyate jñānaṁ rajaso lobha eva cha\npramāda-mohau tamaso bhavato ’jñānam eva cha#From the mode of goodness arises knowledge, from the mode of passion arises greed, and from the mode of ignorance arise negligence and delusion.#sattvāt—from the mode of goodness

BG 14.18#ऊर्ध्वं गच्छन्ति सत्त्वस्था मध्ये तिष्ठन्ति राजसा:\nजघन्यगुणवृत्तिस्था अधो गच्छन्ति तामसा:#ūrdhvaṁ gachchhanti sattva-sthā madhye tiṣhṭhanti rājasāḥ\njaghanya-guṇa-vṛitti-sthā adho gachchhanti tāmasāḥ#Those situated in the mode of goodness rise upward
column2: those in the mode of passion stay in the middle
column3: and those in the mode of ignorance go downward.#ūrdhvam—upward
column5: sattva-sthāḥ—those situated in the mode of goodness
column8: rājasāḥ—those in the mode of passion
column14: tāmasāḥ—those in the mode of ignorance

column1: BG 14.19#नान्यं गुणेभ्य: कर्तारं यदा द्रष्टानुपश्यति\nगुणेभ्यश्च परं वेत्ति मद्भावं सोऽधिगच्छति#nānyaṁ guṇebhyaḥ kartāraṁ yadā draṣhṭānupaśhyati\nguṇebhyaśh cha paraṁ vetti mad-bhāvaṁ so ’dhigachchhati#When wise persons see that in all work there is no agent of action other than the three guṇas, and they know Me to be transcendental to these guṇas, they attain My divine nature.#na—no

BG 14.20#गुणानेतानतीत्य त्रीन्देही देहसमुद्भवान्\nजन्ममृत्युजरादु:खैर्विमुक्तोऽमृतमश्रुते#guṇān etān atītya trīn dehī deha-samudbhavān\njanma-mṛityu-jarā-duḥkhair vimukto ’mṛitam aśhnute#By transcending the three modes of material nature associated with the body, one becomes free from birth, death, old age, and misery, and attains immortality.#guṇān—the three modes of material nature

BG 14.21#अर्जुन उवाच
कैर्लिङ्गैस्त्रीन्गुणानेतानतीतो भवति प्रभो
किमाचार: कथं चैतांस्त्रीन्गुणानतिवर्तते#arjuna uvācha
kair liṅgais trīn guṇān etān atīto bhavati prabho
kim āchāraḥ kathaṁ chaitāns trīn guṇān ativartate#Arjun inquired: What are the characteristics of those who have gone beyond the three guṇas, O Lord? How do they act? How do they go beyond the bondage of the guṇas?#arjunaḥ uvācha—Arjun inquired

BG 14.22#श्रीभगवानुवाच
प्रकाशं च प्रवृत्तिं च मोहमेव च पाण्डव
न द्वेष्टि सम्प्रवृत्तानि न निवृत्तानि काङ् क्षति
उदासीनवदासीनो गुणैर्यो न विचाल्यते
गुणा वर्तन्त इत्येवं योऽवतिष्ठति नेङ्गते#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha
prakāśhaṁ cha pravṛittiṁ cha moham eva cha pāṇḍava
na dveṣhṭi sampravṛittāni na nivṛittāni kāṅkṣhati
udāsīna-vad āsīno guṇair yo na vichālyate
guṇā vartanta ity evaṁ yo ’vatiṣhṭhati neṅgate#BG 14.22-23:#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha—the Supreme Divine Personality said
column17: guṇaiḥ—to the modes of material nature

column1:
BG 14.23#श्रीभगवानुवाच
प्रकाशं च प्रवृत्तिं च मोहमेव च पाण्डव
न द्वेष्टि सम्प्रवृत्तानि न निवृत्तानि काङ् क्षति
उदासीनवदासीनो गुणैर्यो न विचाल्यते
गुणा वर्तन्त इत्येवं योऽवतिष्ठति नेङ्गते#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha
prakāśhaṁ cha pravṛittiṁ cha moham eva cha pāṇḍava
na dveṣhṭi sampravṛittāni na nivṛittāni kāṅkṣhati
udāsīna-vad āsīno guṇair yo na vichālyate
guṇā vartanta ity evaṁ yo ’vatiṣhṭhati neṅgate#BG 14.22-23:#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha—the Supreme Divine Personality said
column17: guṇaiḥ—to the modes of material nature

column1:
BG 14.24#समदु:खसुख: स्वस्थ: समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चन:
तुल्यप्रियाप्रियो धीरस्तुल्यनिन्दात्मसंस्तुति:
मानापमानयोस्तुल्यस्तुल्यो मित्रारिपक्षयो:
सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी गुणातीत: स उच्यते#sama-duḥkha-sukhaḥ sva-sthaḥ sama-loṣhṭāśhma-kāñchanaḥ
tulya-priyāpriyo dhīras tulya-nindātma-sanstutiḥmānāpamānayos tulyas tulyo mitrāri-pakṣhayoḥ
sarvārambha-parityāgī guṇātītaḥ sa uchyate#BG 14.24-25:#sama—alike
column26: guṇa-atītaḥ—risen above the three modes of material nature

column1:
BG 14.25#समदु:खसुख: स्वस्थ: समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चन:
तुल्यप्रियाप्रियो धीरस्तुल्यनिन्दात्मसंस्तुति:
मानापमानयोस्तुल्यस्तुल्यो मित्रारिपक्षयो:
सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी गुणातीत: स उच्यते#sama-duḥkha-sukhaḥ sva-sthaḥ sama-loṣhṭāśhma-kāñchanaḥ
tulya-priyāpriyo dhīras tulya-nindātma-sanstutiḥmānāpamānayos tulyas tulyo mitrāri-pakṣhayoḥ
sarvārambha-parityāgī guṇātītaḥ sa uchyate#BG 14.24-25:#sama—alike
column26: guṇa-atītaḥ—risen above the three modes of material nature

column1: BG 14.26#मां च योऽव्यभिचारेण भक्तियोगेन सेवते\nस गुणान्समतीत्यैतान्ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते#māṁ cha yo ’vyabhichāreṇa bhakti-yogena sevate\nsa guṇān samatītyaitān brahma-bhūyāya kalpate#Those who serve Me with unalloyed devotion rise above the three modes of material nature and come to the level of the Brahman.#mām—me
column8: guṇān—the three modes of material nature

column1: BG 14.27#ब्रह्मणो हि प्रतिष्ठाहममृतस्याव्ययस्य च\nशाश्वतस्य च धर्मस्य सुखस्यैकान्तिकस्य च#brahmaṇo hi pratiṣhṭhāham amṛitasyāvyayasya cha\nśhāśhvatasya cha dharmasya sukhasyaikāntikasya cha#I am the basis of the formless Brahman, the immortal and imperishable, of eternal dharma, and of unending divine bliss.#brahmaṇaḥ—of Brahman
